name: Commercial Loan Lenders
description_short: Banc of California specializes in commercial real estate lending with loans from $3M-$35M+ for income-producing properties including multifamily, office, retail, and industrial assets.
description_long: Banc of California positions itself as one of the fastest-growing commercial real estate lenders, offering institutional-level financing for professional real estate investors and developers. The company has built a lending practice focused exclusively on income-producing commercial properties rather than consumer lending. Their website emphasizes personalized service from a seasoned team of professionals, suggesting an account-manager-driven relationship model typical of institutional lenders. The company operates with on-balance-sheet lending, meaning they hold loans on their own books rather than immediately selling them, which theoretically allows for more flexible terms. Banc of California serves communities through commercial real estate investment, positioning capital deployment as community development. Their lending footprint and scale suggest they are a regional or national bank with significant commercial real estate expertise. The company explicitly markets growth-oriented financing for acquisition, construction, and expansion of commercial properties, targeting sophisticated borrowers familiar with bridge financing, permanent loans, and structured finance products. Their operational structure and loan sizing indicate institutional-grade lending rather than small business or consumer finance.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Offers loans from $3 million to $35 million+, accommodating larger commercial real estate projects
Provides customizable loan structures including permanent, bridge, and structured finance options
Competitive fixed and floating rates available with flexible prepayment fee structures
Efficient loan approval process designed to accommodate quick closing requirements
On-balance-sheet lending allows for more flexible terms than warehouse lenders
Serves diverse asset types: multifamily, office, retail, industrial, and self-storage properties
Loan terms available up to 10 years with both recourse and nonrecourse loan options
cons: Minimum loan size of $3 million excludes small business owners and smaller investors
Focused exclusively on commercial real estate; no other business lending products offered
Website provides limited transparency on specific rates, actual closing costs, or approval timelines
Requires direct contact via phone or form submission to obtain pricing, making comparison shopping difficult
No mention of specialized lending programs for distressed properties, value-add, or opportunistic strategies
best_for: Commercial real estate investors and developers with projects requiring $3M-$35M+ in financing
Multifamily apartment complex owners seeking bridge or permanent financing
Institutional investors acquiring or refinancing office, retail, and industrial properties
diagnosis: Banc of California is best suited for institutional and professional real estate investors with substantial projects requiring $3M+ in commercial real estate financing. The primary caveat is that this is an institutional lender, not a small business lender or consumer finance company—borrowers should have commercial real estate investment experience and expect an account-manager-based relationship model rather than transparent, pre-set terms.
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 8632 S Sepulveda Blvd, Los Angeles, CA 90045
